r: supnā, sapnā, supn, supan, sapn, sapan
ety: Prk. सुप्पणो and सुप्पणओ; S. स्वप्नः and स्वप्न+कः
def: s.m. Sleep; dreaming, a dream, vision
more: n. m. S. स्वप्त, Pr. sivino . A dream. 1. Supne kī sī māyā jis ko apnī batlāve . Prov. The fleeting shadows of a dream our own possessions seem. 2. Miyāṅ ke miyāṅ gaë, bure bure sapne āë . Prov. My husband gone from me, bad dreams I needs must see. (Misfortunes never come single). 3. Ghaṭat chhin chhin, baṛhat pal pal, jāt na lāve bār, Kahat Kabīr, 'Suno, bhai sādho! supnā hai sansār .' Kabīr. Life but declines as moments grow, life's sands quick running seem. Kabir saith, 'hear, O brother monks! this world is but a dream.' 4. Sobat thī nīko, supno thī dekhat, Mo ko supne kī bāt bhulāë daī re. Saiyāṅ sobat mo ko jagāë daī re . Farrukh Wom. song. I slept so sound, a dream I had, My dream's enjoyment he forbad, My spouse awoke me, 'twas so sad! 5. Umā! kahūṅ maiṅ an-bhav apnā, Sat Harī bhajan, jagat sab supnā . Rāmāyan. sapan dosh , n. m. Pollutio nocturna ; a wet dream; shooting the bisho…
s. m. A dream. سپن دوش sapn-dosh ( स्वप्नदोष ) s. m. Pollutio nocturna. سپنابچاري sapnā-bichār-ī ( स्वप्नविचारी ) s. m. An interpreter of dreams.
ex: تھوڑی دیر کے بعد، اُن کے سپنے سچّے نکلے۔
thoṛī der ke ba'ad, un ke sapne sacce nikle.
After a little while, their dreams became true.
ناخدا بننا ہمیشہ اس کا سپنا تھا۔
nāxudā bannā hameśa us kā sapnā thā.
It was always his dream to become a pilot.
wety: Cognates include Latin somnus, Ancient Greek ὕπνος (húpnos), Old English swefn, and (English sweven).

Sapūt meṅ sab ko sājā hoi, kapūt meṅ kāhū ko nā hai . In good sons all an interest have, in bad no one. 3. Hoi bhale ke an-bhalo, hai dānī ke sūm, Hoi kapūt sapūt ke jyoṅ pāvak meṅ dhūm . Dohāvalī. more: n. f. 1. See bhes 1—4. 2. Face; features ( chehrah , 1, 2). 1. ' Rote kyoṅ ho?' Kahā, 'sūrat hī aisī hai .' Prov. 'What makes you cry,' I said? Says he, 'it is my face so made.' 2. Sūrat meṅ aise, sīrat meṅ aise . Prov. saying. One quality to outward sight, within is hid another quite. 3. Case; predicament; plight ( hālat 1, 2). Nahīṅ ek sūrat pe koī mudām, Usī kī garaz zāt ko hai qāyām . Mīr Hasan. Nothing on earth one state maintains Save He whom He himself sustains. 4. Signs ( āsār , 1-3). 5. Manner ( ḍhaṅg 1-3). 6. ( sūrat-i-fel ; H. kiryā dashā ) ( Gram .) Mood. 7. Means ( shakl , 3). sūrat-i-ikhtiyārī ; S. shaktyarth niyam ( Gram .) Potential mood. sūrat-āshnā , adj. Knowing one by sight only. sūrat-i-āmrah ; S. manumatyarth niyam ; H. bidhi kiryā , ( Gram .) The imperative mood. sūrat bāṅdhnā , v. a. To represent a picture or scene. sūrat badalnā , v. a. To transform; change. sūra…

def: s.f. Remembrance, memory, recollection; thinking (of or upon), thought, consideration, reflection; intelligence; prudence; attention; accuracy; mind, intention, aim, design
more: n. f. S. स्मृति 1. See chīt . 2. Recollection; thought. 1. Na khāne kī surat, na pīne kī surat, sāre din phirtā phire hai . Hin. Wom. 2. Use apne āpe kī to surat hai hī nahīṅ . 3. Sumran kar meṅ, surat na Har meṅ, kaho bhek yeh kaisā? Upar se to sidh ban baiṭhā, bhītar paisā paisā! His beads in hand, no thought of Har, what can this false guise mean? Without a holy seer he sits, on pice his heart is keen. 3. Intention; aim; design. Par triyā, par dhan ke ūpar jo koi sutā dhare hai, Chaubolā. Jab chhūṭe haiṅ prān, piyāre, jāke narak paṛe hai . Another's wife, another's wealth whoso designs to take, His soul, O friend! the body quits, doomed for the Stygian lake. surat uṭhānā yā dharnā , v. a. To fix one's mind on. surat ānā , v. n. s. f. Recollection, consideration, reflection, memory, attention, caution, accuracy.

more: adj. 1. Red. 2. Dear. Āj kal bhāo surkh hai. surkh , n. f. A ghuṅgchī , one-eighth of a māshā. surkh-bādā . The hectic-flush disease; erysipelas. surkh-bed , n. f. 1. A kind of cane. 2. A medicinal plant. surkh-rū , adj. lit . red face. 1. Successful; triumphant. 2. Unabashed (not pale with the consciousness of guilt, nullā culpā pillescens ). surkh-rū h. , v. n. 1. To be victorious. 2. To be exonerated, acquitted. 3. To recover one's good name; to be reinstated. surkh-rūī , n. f. 1. Success. 2. Exoneration; honorable acquittal. surkh o safaid; maidā o shahāb , adj. lit . red and white. 1. Fair; ruddy. 2. Plump; round. surkh h. , v. n. 1. To be red. 2. To be ripe (fruit). 3. To be red-hot. 4. To be flushed, blood-shot; to flare up.

'To have a surḵẖāb's feather on the head'; to be proud, to hold one's head high, be above (one's) work.
